groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Minimum strategic, factual, structural, and stylistic information needed to generate a valid YouTube package.

groups.title: Content & Retention
groups.description: Opening, viewer problem, required content, examples, supporting materials, and pacing.

groups.title: Discovery & Packaging
groups.description: Titles, thumbnail concepts, search positioning, description links, and viewer-facing packaging.

groups.title: Channel, Brand & Production
groups.description: Channel context, presenter style, available equipment, visual assets, and editing capacity.

groups.title: Monetization, Safety & Final Settings
groups.description: Commercial relationships, disclosures, high-stakes subjects, privacy restrictions, and final requirements.

variables.label: Video topic or offer
variables.placeholder: Describe what the video is about, including any product, subject, event, or question it covers.
variables.hint: Give enough context to distinguish this video from other content on the same general topic.

help.what: Give enough context to distinguish this video from other content on the same general topic.
help.why: It establishes the central subject and factual scope of the entire package.
help.example: Describe what the video is about, including any product, subject, event, or question it covers.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Choose the main outcome for this video.
variables.hint: Select the single most important result rather than several competing objectives.

help.what: Select the single most important result rather than several competing objectives.
help.why: The goal determines the video angle, CTA, pacing, and publishing package.
help.example: Choose the main outcome for this video.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Who should watch this, and what do they already know about the topic?
variables.hint: Include experience level, situation, needs, or relevant audience characteristics.

help.what: Include experience level, situation, needs, or relevant audience characteristics.
help.why: Audience knowledge and motivation materially affect explanations, examples, tone, and packaging.
help.example: Who should watch this, and what do they already know about the topic?
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Main viewer takeaway
variables.placeholder: What should viewers understand, remember, believe, or be able to do after watching?
variables.hint: Focus on one central promise or conclusion.

help.what: Focus on one central promise or conclusion.
help.why: It prevents the video from becoming an unfocused collection of loosely connected points.
help.example: What should viewers understand, remember, believe, or be able to do after watching?
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Essential facts
variables.placeholder: Add the facts, features, dates, claims, prices, results, or details that must remain accurate.
variables.hint: Include only confirmed information; unsupported details will not be invented.

help.what: Include only confirmed information; unsupported details will not be invented.
help.why: It provides the approved factual foundation for the script and metadata.
help.example: Add the facts, features, dates, claims, prices, results, or details that must remain accurate.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Video format
variables.placeholder: Choose the closest video format.
variables.hint: Choose the format that best fits the video the package should help create.

help.what: Choose the format that best fits the video the package should help create.
help.why: Different formats require different structures, pacing, packaging, and production notes.
help.example: Choose the closest video format.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Script format
variables.placeholder: Choose how detailed the recording material should be.
variables.hint: Select whether you need a full script, outline, or hybrid deliverable.

help.what: Select whether you need a full script, outline, or hybrid deliverable.
help.why: It controls the main production asset and prevents over- or under-scripting.
help.example: Choose how detailed the recording material should be.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Target duration
variables.placeholder: Choose the intended video length.
variables.hint: The script or outline will be sized to this duration when the topic supports it.

help.what: The script or outline will be sized to this duration when the topic supports it.
help.why: Duration affects depth, pacing, section count, script length, and chapter planning.
help.example: Choose the intended video length.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Example: English, Ukrainian, Spanish, or Brazilian Portuguese.
variables.hint: Specify the language and regional variant if relevant.

help.what: Specify the language and regional variant if relevant.
help.why: The language affects script rhythm, title wording, description style, and speaking-length estimates.
help.example: Example: English, Ukrainian, Spanish, or Brazilian Portuguese.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Choose the overall style.
variables.hint: Use Brand-defined only when you provide brand voice details.

help.what: Use Brand-defined only when you provide brand voice details.
help.why: The tone guides the script, titles, description, CTA, and presenter language.
help.example: Choose the overall style.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Channel context
variables.placeholder: Describe the channel niche, usual audience, content style, or where this video fits.
variables.hint: Leave blank if this is a standalone package and no channel context matters.

help.what: Leave blank if this is a standalone package and no channel context matters.
help.why: It helps align the package with existing audience expectations and channel positioning.
help.example: Describe the channel niche, usual audience, content style, or where this video fits.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Primary call to action
variables.placeholder: Example: subscribe, watch another video, download a checklist, book a demo, or leave a comment.
variables.hint: Write Auto if the model should choose one proportional CTA from the goal and context.

help.what: Write Auto if the model should choose one proportional CTA from the goal and context.
help.why: A complete package needs one clear next step without stacking competing actions.
help.example: Example: subscribe, watch another video, download a checklist, book a demo, or leave a comment.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Viewer problem or desire
variables.placeholder: Describe the problem, desire, confusion, question, or motivation that makes viewers care.
variables.hint: Use this when the topic needs stronger viewer relevance.

help.what: Use this when the topic needs stronger viewer relevance.
help.why: It sharpens the hook, intro, examples, title angles, and retention map.
help.example: Describe the problem, desire, confusion, question, or motivation that makes viewers care.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Opening approach
variables.placeholder: Choose how the video should open.
variables.hint: Auto selects the strongest opening based on the goal and topic.

help.what: Auto selects the strongest opening based on the goal and topic.
help.why: The opening strongly affects retention and viewer expectation.
help.example: Choose how the video should open.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Required points
variables.placeholder: List points, sections, examples, objections, steps, phrases, or proof that must appear.
variables.hint: Use only for information that must be included.

help.what: Use only for information that must be included.
help.why: It prevents important user-approved material from being omitted from a large package.
help.example: List points, sections, examples, objections, steps, phrases, or proof that must appear.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Things to avoid
variables.placeholder: List claims, phrases, examples, angles, competitors, topics, or production choices to avoid.
variables.hint: Use this for brand, accuracy, privacy, or positioning limits.

help.what: Use this for brand, accuracy, privacy, or positioning limits.
help.why: It prevents the script and package from using unwanted or unsafe material.
help.example: List claims, phrases, examples, angles, competitors, topics, or production choices to avoid.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Sources and reference material
variables.placeholder: Paste source notes, research summaries, approved data, transcript notes, or reference links.
variables.hint: Use only material you are allowed to use; do not paste long copyrighted passages.

help.what: Use only material you are allowed to use; do not paste long copyrighted passages.
help.why: It gives the model supported material for claims and prevents fabricated evidence.
help.example: Paste source notes, research summaries, approved data, transcript notes, or reference links.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Stories and examples
variables.placeholder: Add approved anecdotes, examples, customer situations, demos, or case details.
variables.hint: Mark examples as hypothetical if they are not real or cannot be verified.

help.what: Mark examples as hypothetical if they are not real or cannot be verified.
help.why: Stories and examples make long-form videos more concrete and easier to follow.
help.example: Add approved anecdotes, examples, customer situations, demos, or case details.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Pacing and retention
variables.placeholder: Choose the preferred pacing style.
variables.hint: This controls transitions, section rhythm, and retention devices without encouraging clickbait.

help.what: This controls transitions, section rhythm, and retention devices without encouraging clickbait.
help.why: Retention strategy affects structure and delivery across the entire video.
help.example: Choose the preferred pacing style.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Viewer search intent
variables.placeholder: Describe what viewers might search for, compare, solve, learn, or decide before watching.
variables.hint: Leave blank for videos that are not search-led.

help.what: Leave blank for videos that are not search-led.
help.why: Search intent shapes titles, description wording, keyword themes, and structure.
help.example: Describe what viewers might search for, compare, solve, learn, or decide before watching.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Target keywords
variables.placeholder: List approved primary and secondary topic phrases, product terms, or search phrases.
variables.hint: Use natural topic phrases rather than a keyword-stuffing list.

help.what: Use natural topic phrases rather than a keyword-stuffing list.
help.why: It guides discovery metadata while keeping title and description wording relevant.
help.example: List approved primary and secondary topic phrases, product terms, or search phrases.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Preferred title angles
variables.placeholder: List preferred angles: Search-led, Curiosity-led, Benefit-led, Problem and solution, Contrarian, Authority-led, Story-led, Comparison-led.
variables.hint: Multi-select adapted to textarea: list any applicable title angles, separated by commas.

help.what: Multi-select adapted to textarea: list any applicable title angles, separated by commas.
help.why: It guides title variety without forcing a single packaging style.
help.example: List preferred angles: Search-led, Curiosity-led, Benefit-led, Problem and solution, Contrarian, Authority-led, Story-led, Comparison-led.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Number of title options
variables.hint: Choose between 3 and 10 title options.

help.what: Choose between 3 and 10 title options.
help.why: A complete package needs enough title choices for comparison without generating noise.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Thumbnail direction
variables.placeholder: Choose the preferred thumbnail style.
variables.hint: Thumbnail concepts must accurately represent the video.

help.what: Thumbnail concepts must accurately represent the video.
help.why: Thumbnail style affects the visual packaging concepts and title pairing.
help.example: Choose the preferred thumbnail style.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Title and thumbnail limits
variables.placeholder: List words, visual ideas, promises, thumbnail elements, or title styles that must be avoided.
variables.hint: Use this to prevent misleading, off-brand, or overused packaging.

help.what: Use this to prevent misleading, off-brand, or overused packaging.
help.why: It keeps titles and thumbnails accurate, brand-safe, and aligned with constraints.
help.example: List words, visual ideas, promises, thumbnail elements, or title styles that must be avoided.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Description links
variables.placeholder: Paste approved URLs, resources, product links, credits, timestamps, or link labels.
variables.hint: Only supplied links may appear in the description or pinned comment.

help.what: Only supplied links may appear in the description or pinned comment.
help.why: The template must not invent URLs, affiliate links, resources, or credits.
help.example: Paste approved URLs, resources, product links, credits, timestamps, or link labels.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Channel name
variables.placeholder: Example: Creatornivo Studio
variables.hint: Use when the package should reference the channel directly.

help.what: Use when the package should reference the channel directly.
help.why: Channel name can affect intro wording, description, and CTA phrasing.
help.example: Example: Creatornivo Studio
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Brand voice
variables.placeholder: Describe voice traits, phrases to use, phrases to avoid, or a short style note.
variables.hint: Use this when the channel has an established voice.

help.what: Use this when the channel has an established voice.
help.why: It helps the script and metadata fit the creator’s existing brand without imitating another creator.
help.example: Describe voice traits, phrases to use, phrases to avoid, or a short style note.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Presentation style
variables.placeholder: Choose how the video will be presented.
variables.hint: Auto chooses a suitable style from the brief and available resources.

help.what: Auto chooses a suitable style from the brief and available resources.
help.why: Presentation style affects script phrasing, production notes, and visual planning.
help.example: Choose how the video will be presented.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Available production resources
variables.placeholder: List available resources: Basic setup only, Smartphone or camera, External microphone, Screen recording, Existing footage, Stock footage, Product demonstration, Slides or graphics, Guest or interview.
variables.hint: Multi-select adapted to textarea: list the resources actually available.

help.what: Multi-select adapted to textarea: list the resources actually available.
help.why: Production notes must be feasible with the user’s actual filming and editing resources.
help.example: List available resources: Basic setup only, Smartphone or camera, External microphone, Screen recording, Existing footage, Stock footage, Product demonstration, Slides or graphics, Guest or interview.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Existing visual assets
variables.placeholder: Describe screenshots, footage, product images, charts, B-roll, logos, demos, or assets available.
variables.hint: Include only assets you can legally use.

help.what: Include only assets you can legally use.
help.why: It allows production notes and thumbnail concepts to use real available visuals.
help.example: Describe screenshots, footage, product images, charts, B-roll, logos, demos, or assets available.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Editing level
variables.placeholder: Choose the realistic editing complexity.
variables.hint: This keeps production notes achievable.

help.what: This keeps production notes achievable.
help.why: Editing capacity affects pacing, visuals, graphics, B-roll, and post-production recommendations.
help.example: Choose the realistic editing complexity.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Video chapters
variables.placeholder: Choose whether to include chapters.
variables.hint: Auto includes chapters only when the structure and duration support them.

help.what: Auto includes chapters only when the structure and duration support them.
help.why: Chapters are useful for longer segmented videos but not every format needs them.
help.example: Choose whether to include chapters.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Commercial relationship
variables.placeholder: Select any commercial relationship.
variables.hint: This controls sponsorship, affiliate, owned-product, employer, client, and paid-promotion handling.

help.what: This controls sponsorship, affiliate, owned-product, employer, client, and paid-promotion handling.
help.why: Commercial interests affect disclosure, description wording, CTA, and claims.
help.example: Select any commercial relationship.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Promotion details
variables.placeholder: Describe the product, sponsor, affiliate link, owned offer, compensation, or relationship details.
variables.hint: Include only confirmed information that may be disclosed.

help.what: Include only confirmed information that may be disclosed.
help.why: Promotional facts and relationship details cannot be invented safely.
help.example: Describe the product, sponsor, affiliate link, owned offer, compensation, or relationship details.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Required disclosure
variables.placeholder: Paste required sponsor, affiliate, employer, client, or paid-promotion wording.
variables.hint: When blank, the output may suggest plain-language disclosure wording for review.

help.what: When blank, the output may suggest plain-language disclosure wording for review.
help.why: Required disclosure wording may be contractual or jurisdiction-specific and cannot be invented confidently.
help.example: Paste required sponsor, affiliate, employer, client, or paid-promotion wording.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Sensitive or regulated topic
variables.placeholder: Select a category only when the video enters a high-stakes subject area.
variables.hint: This enables cautious wording and relevant verification notes without adding a large compliance questionnaire.

help.what: This enables cautious wording and relevant verification notes without adding a large compliance questionnaire.
help.why: High-stakes subjects require different claim handling and safety protections.
help.example: Select a category only when the video enters a high-stakes subject area.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Relevant jurisdiction
variables.placeholder: For example: United States, European Union, Ukraine, California, or England and Wales.
variables.hint: Add this only when laws, regulations, elections, financial rules, or local guidance matter.

help.what: Add this only when laws, regulations, elections, financial rules, or local guidance matter.
help.why: Legal and regulatory information can differ substantially between jurisdictions.
help.example: For example: United States, European Union, Ukraine, California, or England and Wales.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Privacy restrictions
variables.placeholder: Identify private names, faces, locations, client details, children, records, or data that must be removed or anonymized.
variables.hint: Do not paste unnecessary sensitive personal data into this field.

help.what: Do not paste unnecessary sensitive personal data into this field.
help.why: The user knows which real people or confidential details require protection.
help.example: Identify private names, faces, locations, client details, children, records, or data that must be removed or anonymized.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Add any final instructions that are not already covered by another field.
variables.hint: Use this as the only general miscellaneous field.

help.what: Use this as the only general miscellaneous field.
help.why: It accommodates legitimate project-specific constraints without expanding the standard form.
help.example: Add any final instructions that are not already covered by another field.
help.avoid: Avoid vague inputs, unsupported claims, invented facts, conflicting instructions, or irrelevant context.
